skill.name: pydicom
skill.description: Guidance and helper scripts for reading, inspecting, anonymizing, converting, and writing DICOM medical imaging files with pydicom.
skill.summary: Work with DICOM metadata, pixel data, transfer syntaxes, and local conversion workflows using pydicom.
skill.author: K-Dense-AI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review shows most are false positives from Markdown backticks, DICOM UID values, and documentation URLs. No evidence found of command execution, network exfiltration, credential harvesting, or malicious prompt injection. The meaningful risk is privacy-related: the included anonymization and metadata scripts can expose PHI if users treat them as complete clinical de-identification tools.

medium_findings.title: Incomplete DICOM De-identification Guidance
medium_findings.description: The anonymization helper removes or replaces a fixed list of top-level PHI tags and leaves UID anonymization commented out. DICOM files can contain PHI in private tags, sequences, UIDs, free-text fields, and burned-in pixel data, so users may share data that is not fully de-identified.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code clearly performs partial PHI removal and explicitly leaves UID replacement optional. The risk is privacy leakage from incomplete de-identification, not malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: Metadata Export Can Write Sensitive Patient Data
medium_findings.description: The metadata extraction script serializes DICOM file metadata, including patient and study fields, and can write it to a user-selected output file. This is expected functionality, but it needs a privacy warning because metadata may contain PHI.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script intentionally extracts broad DICOM metadata and writes it locally when requested. Legitimate use is clear, but PHI exposure is plausible in normal use.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Findings Are Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution hits are Markdown code fences and inline code examples for installing packages or using pydicom APIs. No evidence found that the skill executes shell commands automatically.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are documentation snippets, not executable skill code. The actual Python scripts use argparse and pydicom APIs without subprocess or shell execution.

low_findings.title: Static Network And Weak Crypto Findings Are DICOM References
low_findings.description: The hardcoded IP and weak cryptography alerts correspond to DICOM UID numeric identifiers, compression names, or documentation URLs. No evidence found of outbound network requests or cryptographic security decisions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The numeric dotted strings are standard DICOM transfer syntax UIDs, not IP addresses. The URLs are documentation references and there is no code that performs network access.

low_findings.title: Local File Writes Are User-Directed Outputs
low_findings.description: The filesystem findings are expected local outputs for anonymized DICOM files, converted images, and extracted metadata. The scripts do not hide writes or target sensitive system paths, but users should choose output paths carefully.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The writes are explicit outputs controlled by command-line arguments. There is minor operational risk from overwriting or storing PHI locally, but no malicious file behavior was found.

dangerous_patterns.title: Fixed PHI Tag Allowlist
dangerous_patterns.description: Anonymization is based on a predefined tag list and does not recursively inspect every possible PHI location. This can miss private tags, sequence content, or identifiers outside the list.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The allowlist and loop are directly visible in the script. The remaining risk depends on the content of each DICOM file.

dangerous_patterns.title: UID De-identification Disabled By Default
dangerous_patterns.description: UID replacement is present only as commented guidance. Study, series, and SOP instance identifiers may remain linkable unless users change the script.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code comments explicitly show UID anonymization is optional and not executed. This creates a clear re-identification concern.

content.value_statement: DICOM files are complex, sensitive, and difficult to inspect without specialized tooling. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through local pydicom workflows for metadata, pixels, conversion, and de-identification review.
actual_capabilities: Read DICOM files and explain common pydicom access patterns.
Extract patient, study, series, image, and file meta fields.
Convert DICOM pixel data to PNG, JPEG, TIFF, or BMP outputs.
Apply basic windowing, color conversion, and frame selection guidance.
Describe transfer syntaxes, compression handlers, and common DICOM tags.
Provide helper scripts for local anonymization, metadata export, and image conversion.
limitations: The anonymization helper is not a complete clinical de-identification solution.
Compressed DICOM support depends on installed handlers such as pylibjpeg or GDCM.
The skill does not include PACS networking, DICOMweb clients, or cloud storage integration.
Medical image interpretation still requires qualified clinical review.

use_cases.title: Inspect a Study Before Analysis
use_cases.description: Extract core metadata, confirm modality and dimensions, and identify transfer syntax before running downstream analysis.

use_cases.title: Prepare Images for Visualization
use_cases.description: Convert DICOM pixel data into standard image formats while handling windowing, frames, and color space issues.

use_cases.title: Review De-identification Needs
use_cases.description: Use the anonymization guidance as a starting point, then verify remaining PHI risks before sharing datasets.

prompt_templates.title: Read Basic Metadata
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me inspect a DICOM file with pydicom. Show the safest steps to read patient, study, modality, rows, columns, and transfer syntax fields.

prompt_templates.title: Convert One Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Guide me through converting one DICOM file to PNG with pydicom and Pillow. Include windowing checks and common error handling.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Anonymization
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my DICOM de-identification workflow. Identify PHI fields, UID risks, private tags, sequence content, and burned-in annotation checks.

prompt_templates.title: Process a Series
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a pydicom workflow for a directory of CT slices. Sort the series, validate spacing, build a volume, and report data quality issues.

output_examples.input: A user asks how to inspect an MRI DICOM file before analysis.
output: A step-by-step plan to read the file locally.
A checklist of metadata fields to verify.
Warnings about PHI in patient and study fields.

output_examples.input: A user wants to convert a multi-frame ultrasound file to images.
output: Image format guidance for PNG or JPEG output.
Troubleshooting notes for color conversion and missing handlers.

output_examples.input: A user plans to share anonymized DICOM files with researchers.
output: A privacy review checklist for tags, UIDs, private elements, and pixels.
A reminder to validate de-identification with approved institutional policy.

best_practices: Work on local copies and preserve original DICOM files before modifying metadata or pixels.
Verify de-identification with domain policy before sharing any healthcare imaging data.
Check transfer syntax and installed pixel handlers before building large conversion pipelines.
anti_patterns: Do not assume removing PatientName and PatientID fully anonymizes a DICOM file.
Do not publish extracted metadata without checking for PHI in free-text and private fields.
Do not use lossy compression for diagnostic images unless clinical requirements allow it.

faq.question: Can this skill diagnose medical images?
faq.answer: No. It helps process DICOM files and metadata, but medical interpretation requires qualified clinical review.

faq.question: Does it support compressed DICOM files?
faq.answer: Yes, when compatible pixel handlers are installed. Some syntaxes require packages such as pylibjpeg, pillow, or python-gdcm.

faq.question: Is the anonymization script enough for research sharing?
faq.answer: No. It is a starting point only. Review UIDs, private tags, sequences, free text, and burned-in pixel annotations.

faq.question: Can it convert DICOM files to common image formats?
faq.answer: Yes. The helper script supports PNG, JPEG, TIFF, and BMP outputs from local DICOM pixel data.

faq.question: Does the skill send DICOM files over the network?
faq.answer: No evidence found of network transfer code. The included scripts operate on local input and output paths.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
